response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced generalist engineer (18+ years) with strong TypeScript/Node.js skills, production cloud experience, and recent hands-on exposure to MCP servers and agentic-workflow tooling, which maps directly to a key technology in this role. However, the role requires 8+ years building production AI-driven solutions, and his GenAI experience appears limited to roughly six months at re:cinq. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI, which are core to the job. His teaching, workshop, and consulting background suggests he could handle customer-facing technical leadership, but the AI depth gap is significant for a staff-level forward deployed engineer.
strengths: Recent hands-on work on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure
18+ years of broad production engineering across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Proven mentoring and stakeholder-facing skills via workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~6 months of GenAI work)
No demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI)
No evidence of building AI evaluation pipelines, observability frameworks, or data pipelines for unstructured data
